title: podmans search tags reverse order
body:
### Feature request description

I would like to have a flag on `podman search --list-tags --order=newest` or similar that shows the newest tags first.  Currently the `--list-tags` shows the oldest first.  I would guess this is not as useful for most people.  Most people want to see the latest ones first.  It would be nice to have this be the default (showing the most recent tags first).

### Suggest potential solution

When a user runs:
```podman search --list-tags whatever```
The results shows are the newest tags.  If this would be a undesirable effect, then instead add a flag to reverse the order.
```podman search --list-tags --order=newest whatever```
This would result in the latest pushed tags showing first.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
Hello,
Can I work on this issue?
If so I would like to work on it
See the PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26338 this cannot be implemented in the registry protocol

Thanks for trying.

BLUF, I guess they dropped the Digest from the results in v2 claiming it was too computationally expensive.
So, I guess the below options... are not an option.  

I guess the Docker CLI has this:
`docker buildx imagetools inspect --format "{{json .Manifest}}" registry | jq -r .digest`

Which supposedly get's the info without having to pull the image.

The below comments **do not seem to be an option** since since v2 search no longer has Digest, but leaving it here anyway:
Is it possible to just list the tags that have the same digest then?  For example:
`podman search --list-tags --matching "latest" registry`
Ideally returning (currently):
```
sha256:61349442e9c3dc07fd06ffa6a4b622bc28960952b6b3adafcb58fa268ce92e70
latest
3
3.0
3.0.0
```

Or add {{.Digest}} under the `--format` option for search?
